source.title: Biosensor and machine learning-aided engineering of an amaryllidaceae enzyme

initial_request.research_problem: Slow biocatalyst engineering is a major challenge for industry-scale biomanufacturing of therapeutic alkaloids.
initial_request.known_context: Therapeutic alkaloids are valuable but biocatalyst engineering is slow. A biosensor-machine learning stack is proposed to accelerate development.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Slow biocatalyst engineering hinders industrial biomanufacturing of therapeutic alkaloids.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for high-throughput screening and efficient enzyme design methods.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A biosensor-machine learning technology stack can accelerate biocatalyst development for Amaryllidaceae enzymes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose to develop a biosensor for 4NB and use ML to guide enzyme engineering.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test RamR as starting point for biosensor development.

evidence.quote_or_span: A major challenge to achieving industry-scale biomanufacturing of therapeutic alkaloids is the slow process of biocatalyst engineering.
Here, we propose an efficient biosensor-machine learning technology stack for biocatalyst development, which we apply to engineer an Amaryllidaceae enzyme in Escherichia coli.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can RamR be engineered into a selective 4NB biosensor?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Wild-type RamR shows slight responsiveness to 4NB and norbelladine, indicating potential for directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Characterize wild-type RamR response to 4NB and norbelladine.
evolution_trajectory.observation: RamR yielded 3.8-fold and 4.4-fold fluorescence increases for 4NB and norbelladine, respectively.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Low selectivity; proceed to directed evolution to improve specificity.

evidence.quote_or_span: RamR was found to be slightly responsive to both 4NB and its immediate precursor norbelladine, yielding 3.8-fold and 4.4-fold increases in fluorescence, respectively

evolution_trajectory.state_before: RamR has low selectivity for 4NB over norbelladine.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to improve selectivity and sensitivity for 4NB.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Directed evolution via site-saturated mutagenesis and SELIS screening can yield a specific 4NB biosensor.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform site-saturated mutagenesis on RamR and screen with SELIS.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Round 1 yields variant 4NB1.2 (K63T, L66M) with 20-fold selectivity for 4NB over norbelladine.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Further evolution with counter-selection to enhance specificity and sensitivity.

evidence.quote_or_span: 2: K63T and L66M) displayed a 20-fold selectivity for 4NB over norbelladine (Supplementary Fig.

the K63T substitution repositions the hydroxyl group at position 3 of 4NB to hydrogen bond with the wild-type Y59 residue, while the L66M substitution strengthens a hydrophobic pocket around the 4'-O-Methyl group of 4NB

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need higher specificity and sensitivity for screening applications.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Additional rounds of evolution with counter-selection can yield a highly selective and sensitive biosensor.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform further directed evolution with counter-selection against norbelladine.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variant 4NB2.1 obtained with >80-fold preference for 4NB over norbelladine and EC50=20 μM, ~10-fold more sensitive than HPLC.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use biosensor to screen Nb4OMT variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: the 4NB2.1 sensor was highly selective for 4NB over norbelladine, displaying an over 80-fold preference for the former
the 4NB2.1 sensor is approximately 10-fold more sensitive than the HPLC method, making it well-suited for screening transplanted biosynthetic enzymes from plants
2: K63T and L66M) displayed a 20-fold selectivity for 4NB over norbelladine (Supplementary Fig.
To identify crystallization conditions of the Nb4OMT variant with triple mutations (E36P + G40E + A53M), 20 mg/ml purified enzyme samples were directly used in sparse matrix screening.

The following day, 20 $\mu$ L of each culture was then used to inoculate six separate wells in a 2-mL 96-deep-well plate (Corning, P-DW-20-C-S) sealed with an AeraSeal film (Excel Scientific) containing 900 $\mu$ L LB medium, one for each test ligand and a solvent control.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Error-prone PCR failed to improve Nb4OMT.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a more effective design strategy.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A structure-based ML model trained on protein microenvironments can predict beneficial mutations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop MutComputeX, a 3DResNet trained on ~2.3M microenvironments including protein-ligand interfaces.
evolution_trajectory.observation: MutComputeX achieved 67.3% wild-type accuracy and can predict mutations.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use MutComputeX to design Nb4OMT variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: A structure-based residual neural network (MutComputeX) is subsequently developed and used to generate activity-enriched variants of a plant methyltransferase, which are rapidly screened with the biosensor.
A total of 2,569,256 microenvironments were sampled from 22,584 protein sequences and split 90:10 to generate our training and test set splits
The ensembled 3DResNet model (MutComputeX) had an overall wild-type accuracy of 67.3%

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MutComputeX is available for variant design.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which mutations will improve Nb4OMT activity and stability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations predicted by MutComputeX on docked AlphaFold2 structure will enhance activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design 22 Nb4OMT variants using MutComputeX on AlphaFold2 structure docked with SAH and norbelladine.

parameters.goals: stabilizing protein away from active site and investigating point mutations differing between docked and apo structures

evolution_trajectory.observation: 22 variants designed, including A53M and E36P-G40E.

evidence.quote_or_span: Mutations were designed with two goals: stabilizing the protein away from the active site and investigating point mutations where predictions differed between the docked and apo protein structures.
Active site context of Nb4OMT $^{E36P/G40E/A53M}$ in complex with S-Adenosyl-L-homocysteine (SAH) and docked with norbelladine.
0 was used to dock norbelladine into the crystal structure of Nb4OMT $^{E36P/G40E/A53M}$ with SAH and $Ca^{2+}$ already in the active site (based on Fo-Fc electron densities; Supplementary Fig.
e Active site context of Nb4OMT $^{E36P/G40E/A53M}$ in complex with S-Adenosyl-L-homocysteine (SAH) and docked with norbelladine.
we determined the structure of the Nb4OMT $^{E36P/G40E/A53M}$ variant in complex with S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine (SAH) at 2.4 Å resolution
Coordinates for the complex structure of Nb4OMT $^{E36P/G40E/A53M}$ with S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine (SAH) has been deposited in the Protein Data Bank (PDB) as 8UKE.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants with A53M will outperform wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen 22 variants with biosensor across three temperatures and two substrate concentrations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: A53M consistently produced signal above wild-type; H17K, H17R, S159E, V203E, E36P-G40E also above wild-type in some conditions. Double/triple mutants with A53M gave greatest signals.

evidence.quote_or_span: In all tested conditions, the A53M mutation consistently produced a fluorescent signal significantly above the wild-type enzyme

In all tested conditions, the A53M mutation consistently produced a fluorescent signal significantly above the wild-type enzyme, while the H17K, H17R, S159E, V203E, and E36P-G40E substitutions produced signals above wild-type in at least one tested condition (Supplementary Fig.

Double and triple mutants incorporating the H17K, A53M, S159E, V203E, and E36P-G40E substitutions were generated and screened; as with the initial screens, variants bearing the A53M mutation produced the greatest signals (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: A53M and E36P-G40E-A53M show promising fluorescence signals.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do these variants actually improve product titer and specificity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: LC/MS will confirm increased 4NB and reduced norbelladine and off-product.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform LC/MS on E36P-G40E, A53M, and E36P-G40E-A53M variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: E36P-G40E-A53M increased 4NB by 60%, decreased norbelladine 17-fold, reduced 3'-O-methylnorbelladine 3-fold.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze importance of docking for A53M prediction.

evidence.quote_or_span: the level of 4NB product increased by 60% while the level of remnant norbelladine decreased 17-fold
the A53M mutation reduced levels of the 3'-O-Methyl-norbelladine off-product by about 3-fold

carried out on supernatants from the E36P-G40E, A53M, and E36P-G40E-A53M variants, and in agreement with our fluorescence-based assay the level of 4NB product increased by 60% while the level of remnant norbelladine decreased 17-fold (Fig.
Furthermore, the A53M mutation reduced levels of the 3'-O-Methyl-norbelladine off-product by about 3-fold (Supplementary Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: A53M and triple mutant show improved product titer.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the kinetic and stability improvements?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A53M increases kcat/Km and Tm; triple mutant further increases Tm.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure steady-state kinetics and thermal stability.

tool_or_method.name: steady-state kinetics and thermal shift assay

evolution_trajectory.observation: A53M increased kcat/Km ~2-fold (kcat >2.1-fold), Tm by 1.7°C; triple mutant Tm increased by 5.6°C. Substrate inhibition noted for A53M and triple mutant.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Solve crystal structure to understand mechanism.

evidence.quote_or_span: The A53M substitution increased $k_{cat}/K_{m}$ by a factor of about 2, due to a >2.1-fold increase in $k_{cat}$ , and increased the $T_{m}$ by 1.7 °C
a 5.6 °C increase in $T_{m}$ relative to the wild-type Nb4OMT enzyme
The Nb4OMT $^{E36P/G40E/A53M}$ triple substitutions appeared to have $k_{cat}$ and $K_{m}$ values similar to the Nb4OMT $^{A53M}$ single mutant, but a 5.

To further understand the mechanism behind beneficial mutations, we characterized the steady state kinetic and thermal properties of NbOMT bearing the A53M substitution alone or in combination with the E36P and G40E substitutions.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do mutations improve activity and stability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Crystal structure will reveal structural basis for improvements.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Solve crystal structure of Nb4OMT E36P/G40E/A53M with SAH.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Structure solved at 2.4 Å; overall fold similar to AlphaFold2 except N-terminal region; homodimer observed. Active site differs from AlphaFold2 due to missing metal ions/ligands.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze structural basis for improved activity and stability.

evidence.quote_or_span: we determined the structure of the Nb4OMT $^{E36P/G40E/A53M}$ variant in complex with S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine (SAH) at 2.4 Å resolution
The overall fold of the protein was almost identical to the predicted AlphaFold2 structure, except for the N-terminal region
Global structure of Nb4OMT $^{E36P/G40E/A53M}$ solved in 2.4 A resolution.
Comparison of N-termini of Nb4OMT crystal structure (orange) and wild-type Nb4OMT AlphaFold2 structure (blue).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the molecular mechanisms of A53M and E36P-G40E?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A53M inserts into hydrophobic pocket; E36P-G40E preserves salt bridge and caps helix; Ca2+ positions substrate.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze crystal structure and dock norbelladine.
evolution_trajectory.observation: A53M inserts methionine into hydrophobic pocket (Trp50, Tyr81, Tyr108); E36P-G40E shifts glutamate to preserve salt bridge with Lys118 and proline caps helix; Ca2+ coordinates substrate; sulfur-π interaction may restrict rotation and improve specificity.

evidence.quote_or_span: The A53M substitution inserts a larger hydrophobic methionine inside the hydrophobic pocket formed by Trp50, Tyr81, and Tyr108
The E36P-G40E double mutant shifts a glutamate from position 36 to position 40 and thereby preserves the salt bridge interaction with Lys118 while proline capping the alpha helix

A sulfur- $\pi$ interaction between the catechol group of norbelladine and Methionine 53 may also restrict the rotation of the catechol group, thereby reducing the cross-methylation of the 3' position and improving specificity
Local context of the A53M mutant residue.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Successful engineering achieved, but limitations exist.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the limitations of the approach?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Only 22 variants tested; manual curation; reliance on AlphaFold2 and docking accuracy; AlphaFold2 model inaccurate in N-terminal region and missed Ca2+.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Identified limitations: only 22 variants tested, manual curation required, AlphaFold2 N-terminal inaccuracy, missing Ca2+ in model.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Future work could address these limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: Interestingly, the active site of the solved structure differs from AlphaFold2 models, likely due to lack of the metal ions, ligands, and cofactors.
While the recently described AlphaFill tool could potentially address this issue for some models, it did not incorporate the norbelladine substrate or provide a more accurate model of Nb4OMT

metrics: Substrate consumption (17-fold decrease in remnant norbelladine)
success_verification.final_conclusion: The biosensor-ML stack successfully engineered Nb4OMT variants with improved activity, specificity, and stability. The A53M mutation was key, and its prediction required ligand-aware ML. Crystal structure provided mechanistic insights.
limitations: Reliance on AlphaFold2 and docking accuracy
AlphaFold2 model inaccurate in N-terminal region and missed Ca2+
Substrate inhibition observed but not fully modeled
